title: Workspace Minimal Setup Default
goal: Implement #452 by making public setup guidance minimal or recommendation-driven instead of implying full-stack install is the normal default.
non_goals: Do not remove the full preset.
Do not require recommendation analysis when a user explicitly selects a preset.
Do not implement a broad feature-flag framework.

intent.outcome: Public docs no longer imply full-stack install is the normal default, and a new repo has a smallest-viable or recommendation-driven setup path.
intent.constraints: Keep existing full installs working and keep explicit memory/planning/full presets available.
intent.latitude: Choose whether the smallest slice is docs-only, setup recommendation output, or a minimal preset alias if that stays small.
intent.escalation: Escalate if satisfying #452 requires incompatible init semantics or broad configuration machinery.

execution.next_step: Archive #452 and promote #453 for the context-router family slice.
execution.proof: README/setup tests as changed, workspace CLI tests, ruff, proof selector, and planning doctor.

invariants: Explicit full installs continue to work.
Memory, planning, and full remain available by name.
The default public setup path favors the smallest viable footprint or a recommendation.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: #450: make advanced features opt-in through feature tiers and merged surfaces.

required_continuation.required follow-on detail: #453-#460 plus #467/#469/#471 remain in the feature-tier and surface-merge lane.
required_continuation.activation trigger: after #452 closeout, promote the next #450 child indicated by validation

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: README now shows memory as the smallest useful preset and frames planning/full as explicit choices; defaults install_profiles now recommends memory/planning/full by problem fit and combined install no longer names full as the primary path.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: #453 should merge compact context commands into a context-router family in docs/output labels.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: A docs/defaults-only slice was enough; changing install semantics or repo default_preset would add unnecessary migration risk.
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q; uv run ruff check src tests packages/planning/src packages/planning/tests; uv run agentic-workspace proof --target . --changed README.md src/agentic_workspace/cli.py tests/test_workspace_cli.py .agentic-workspace/planning/state.toml .agentic-workspace/planning/execplans/workspace-minimal-setup-default.plan.json --format json; uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json; uv run agentic-workspace defaults --section root_cli_authority --format json; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Make minimal or recommendation-driven setup the public default path.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Keep full available and do not break existing explicit preset usage.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Whether the first slice is docs-only, recommendation output, or a compact minimal/routing-only setup path.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: The chosen path requires incompatible install behavior or broad feature-flag/config machinery.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Make the package streamlined, lightweight, frictionless, cheap, modular, and small while preserving leverage.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: A new repo should begin from the smallest viable layer unless evidence justifies memory, planning, or full.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Does setup feel cheaper and less product-heavy after feature tiers are visible?

active_milestone.scope: Keep this execution thread bounded to the promoted TODO item.

immediate_next_action: Inspect README setup guidance, init/setup commands, module registry tiers, and #452 acceptance criteria.
invariants: Explicit full installs continue to work.
Memory, planning, and full remain available by name.
Default public setup guidance does not imply full-stack install.
validation_commands: uv run ruff check src tests packages/planning/src packages/planning/tests

uv run agentic-workspace proof --target . --changed .agentic-workspace/planning/state.toml .agentic-workspace/planning/execplans/workspace-minimal-setup-default.plan.json README.md src/agentic_workspace/cli.py tests/test_workspace_cli.py --format json
uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json
completion_criteria: Public docs no longer imply full-stack install is the normal default.
A new repo has a smallest-viable setup path.
Recommendation output names why memory, planning, or full is justified, or the docs clearly route users to explicit preset selection.
Full preset is framed as opt-in for repos with both durable-memory and active-planning needs.

execution_summary.outcome delivered: Public setup guidance and defaults now start from the smallest fitting preset and frame full as opt-in when both memory and planning are justified.
execution_summary.validation confirmed: CLI tests, ruff, proof selection, planning doctor, root CLI authority review, install-profile defaults dogfooding, and planning-surface checks passed.
execution_summary.post-work posterity capture: README and defaults install_profiles carry durable setup guidance; tests guard the recommendation order and full opt-in wording.

proof_report.validation proof: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q; uv run ruff check src tests packages/planning/src packages/planning/tests; uv run agentic-workspace proof --target . --changed README.md src/agentic_workspace/cli.py tests/test_workspace_cli.py .agentic-workspace/planning/state.toml .agentic-workspace/planning/execplans/workspace-minimal-setup-default.plan.json --format json; uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json; uv run agentic-workspace defaults --section root_cli_authority --format json; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py
proof_report.proof achieved now: Public setup no longer implies full-stack install as the normal default.
proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: README uses --preset memory as the smallest useful shared core; defaults install_profiles gives memory/planning/full recommendation order and combined_install no longer names full as primary.

closure_check.why this decision is honest: The bounded #452 slice is complete, but parent #450 remains open for context-router and surface-merge work.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: README uses --preset memory as the smallest useful shared core; defaults install_profiles recommends memory, planning, then full by problem fit.
closure_check.reopen trigger: Public docs or defaults again present --preset full as the normal setup default.

discard.summary: No migration note is needed because explicit install semantics did not change.

continuation.summary: Promote #453 to present start, summary, report, defaults, and preflight as one context-router family.

drift_log: 2026-04-27: Promoted from TODO direct-task shape into an execplan.
2026-04-27: Completed #452 with smallest-viable setup guidance in README and defaults install_profiles.

intent_satisfaction.original intent: Make minimal or recommendation-driven setup the public default path.
intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: #452 changed README and defaults install_profiles so setup starts from the smallest fitting preset and full is framed as opt-in.
